reviews.name: accounts/109853746269412609224/locations/8137682330194139512/reviews/ChZDSUhNMG9nS0VJQ0FnTURnbzdPNmNnEAE

reviews.comment:
En realidad quiero hacer un comentario acerca de las respuestas de parte de la empresa, que estoy leyendo aquí, dirigida a los usuarios que dejan su comentario. Al no poner una S al final en la palabra NO, cambia el sentido de lo que se quiere expresar al escribir. La forma correcta de escribir sería "nos es de importancia su experiencia" en lugar de decir "no es de importancia su experiencia" Lo bueno de escribir correctamente para evitar confusiones y desagrado hacia quien reclama algo justo para él. Con esa respuesta mal escrita parece que a la empresa le importara un comino lo que haya sucedido.

(Translated by Google)
I actually want to comment on the company's responses, which I'm reading here, directed at users who leave comments. By not adding an 's' to the end of the word 'no,' the meaning of the message is changed. The correct way to write it would be "your experience is important to us" instead of "your experience is not important." The point of writing correctly is to avoid confusion and upsetting those who are claiming something they deserve. With that poorly written response, it seems like the company couldn't care less about what happened.

reviewReply.comment: Muchas Gracias Sra. Márquez Seguiremos mejorando para que no nos vuelva a pasar

I waited 15 minutes for someone to come and help me. I don't know if the attendant fell asleep or what happened. The security guard was also asleep in his car. It was a disaster. I don't know why they're open at night if they don't have competent staff.

reviews.comment:
Fui a comprar algo para tomar, tuve que recorrerme todos los playeros para conseguir la llave del baño, que la tenia el de seguridad (bastante mala onda cuando me abrio). Solo abren el de discapacitados que esta sucio. Dicen que no son baños publicos... Pero es una estacion de servicio, y yo consumi.

(Translated by Google)
I went to buy something to drink, and I had to go through all the gas station attendants to get the bathroom key, which the security guard had (he was pretty rude when he let me in). They only open the accessible restroom, which is filthy. They say they're not public restrooms... But it's a gas station, and I bought something.

reviews.comment:
El playero que me atendió a la noche tarde de terror. No había nadie más que yo, cargué nafta y me olvidé de pedirle limpiar el vidrio, le pedí que lo hago cuando pagué y de mala manera empieza a hacerlo y me dice  "10 minutos al pedo me tuviste". Me parece que no es manera de tratar al cliente cuando yo encima me disculpé y quise darle una buena propina para compensar la "molestia" ni siquiera me la quiso agarrar de lo enojado que estaba. Lo podría entender en hora pico, pero a la 1 de la mañana cuando lo único que estaba haciendo era charlar con el de seguridad me parece que es no querer laburar...

(Translated by Google)
The gas station attendant who served me late at night was awful. I was the only one there, I filled up my tank, and I forgot to ask him to clean the windshield. I asked him to do it after I paid, and he rudely started cleaning it and said, "You kept me waiting for 10 minutes." I don't think that's any way to treat a customer, especially since I apologized and wanted to give him a good tip to compensate for the "inconvenience." He wouldn't even take it because he was so angry. I could understand it during rush hour, but at 1 a.m., when all he was doing was chatting with security, it seems like he just didn't want to work.

reviewReply.comment: Buenos días la agradezco que se tome el tiempo para indicar esta situación y la verdad no es de suma importancia para mejorar este tipo de cuestiones. Muchas gracias por su opinion
